steps.title: Map the three parts of the text
steps.content: The text has three distinct parts: (1) an unresolved question — how deep human activity affects carbon and nitrogen in soil; (2) Okolo and colleagues' hypothesis — that land-use differences affect carbon and nitrogen below the topsoil layer; (3) a finding signaled by "though" — that carbon and nitrogen dropped to comparably low levels beyond 30 cm across all land-use types, which contradicts the hypothesis.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "It describes a phenomenon that scientists do not fully understand, explains a research team's hypothesis about that phenomenon, and then describes a finding that led the team to refine the hypothesis.": The finding did not lead the team to refine their hypothesis — the text simply states the conflicting result with no mention of refinement. ✗
  • "It introduces an unresolved scientific question, presents a research team's hypothesis pertaining to that question, and then describes an observation made by the team that conflicts with that hypothesis.": Matches all three parts exactly — unresolved question, hypothesis, and a conflicting observation. ✓
  • "It discusses a process that scientists are somewhat unclear about, introduces competing hypotheses about that process, and then explains how a research team concluded that one of those hypotheses is likely correct.": The text presents only one hypothesis, not competing hypotheses, and no conclusion about which is correct is drawn. ✗
  • "It explains a hypothesis that has been the subject of scientific debate, discusses how a research team tested that hypothesis, and then presents data the team collected that validate the hypothesis.": The hypothesis is not framed as a subject of debate, and the data contradict the hypothesis rather than validate it. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
